TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: told by Giɫgiɫaw of Vaɫulu village

S.FORM: nu seman puvasan dangdangan anan, sana ɫama-i, sana qusuli.
S.PHON: nu səman puvasan daŋdaŋan anan, sana ʎama-i, sana qusuɭi.
S.FORM: nu seman puvasan dangdangan anan, sana ljama-i, sana qusuli.
S.PHON: nu səman puvasan daŋdaŋan anan, sana ʎama-i, sana qusuɭi.
S.TRANSL: TARO: When we make a taro field, we clear the ground and burn the vegetation and dig out the roots.

S.FORM: nu qinusul anga, nu sariqsiqsiq a kadjunangan, quɫitsan anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: nu qinusuɭ aŋa, nu sariqsiqsiq a kaɉunaŋan, quʎiʦan aŋa sakamaja.
S.FORM: nu qinusul anga, nu sariqsiqsiq a kadjunangan, quljican anga sakamaya.
S.PHON: nu qinusuɭ aŋa, nu sariqsiqsiq a kaɉunaŋan, quʎiʦan aŋa sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: When we have dug out the roots, if the soil is dry we just clear away the burnt vegetation.

S.FORM: ɫakua nu djukel a kadjunangan.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu ɉukəɭ a kaɉunaŋan.
S.FORM: ljakua nu djukel a kadjunangan.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu ɉukəɭ a kaɉunaŋan.
S.TRANSL: But if the soil is sticky

S.FORM: tjara djukulen anan a semualap a qipu izua i ta tjapunguɫan, sana quɫiquɫitsi sa ɫama-i a qinepuan, sana taletalemi tua vasa.
S.PHON: cara ɉukuɭən anan a səmuaɭap a qipu izua i ta capuŋuʎan, sana quʎiquʎiʦi sa ʎama-i a qinəpuan, sana taɭətaɭəmi tua vasa.
S.FORM: tjara djukulen anan a semualap a qipu izua i ta tjapunguljan, sana quljiquljici sa ljama-i a qinepuan, sana taletalemi tua vasa.
S.PHON: ʦara ɉukuɭən anan a səmuaɭap a qipu izua i ta ʦapuŋuʎan, sana quʎiquʎiʦi sa ʎama-i a qinəpuan, sana taɭətaɭəmi tua vasa.
S.TRANSL: we always beat the soil out of the roots, and then clear up and burn the refuse; and then plant the taro.

S.FORM: a siqusulan siumaɫ a upu a pasekaumaɫ.
S.PHON: a siqusuɭan siumaʎ a upu a pasəkaumaʎ.
S.FORM: a siqusulan siumalj a upu a pasekaumalj.
S.PHON: a siqusuɭan siumaʎ a upu a pasəkaumaʎ.
S.TRANSL: When we are digging out the roots we renew the terrace walls in new positions.

S.FORM: nu seman puvaquan namaya tua puvasan a dangdangan anan sana ɫama-i sana kavatsuki.
S.PHON: nu səman puvaquan namaja tua puvasan a daŋdaŋan anan sana ʎama-i sana kavaʦuki.
S.FORM: nu seman puvaquan namaya tua puvasan a dangdangan anan sana ljama-i sana kavacuki.
S.PHON: nu səman puvaquan namaja tua puvasan a daŋdaŋan anan sana ʎama-i sana kavaʦuki.
S.TRANSL: MILLET: When we make a millet field, it’s the same as a taro field; we cut back and burn the vegetation and clear the land.

S.FORM: qau nu mavatsuk itjen, mumaɫ tua upu a zuma nu qaɫan; ɫakua tjatjuruvu a inika mumaumaɫ tua upu nu mavatsuk.
S.PHON: qau nu mavaʦuk icən, mumaʎ tua upu a zuma nu qaʎan; ʎakua cacuruvu a inika mumaumaʎ tua upu nu mavaʦuk.
S.FORM: qau nu mavacuk itjen, mumalj tua upu a zuma nu qaljan; ljakua tjatjuruvu a inika mumaumalj tua upu nu mavacuk.
S.PHON: qau nu mavaʦuk iʦən, mumaʎ tua upu a zuma nu qaʎan; ʎakua ʦaʦuruvu a inika mumaumaʎ tua upu nu mavaʦuk.
S.TRANSL: When we have cleared the land, in some villages they renew the terrace walls; but there are more people who don’t renew the walls after clearing the land.

S.FORM: aitsu a puvaquan inika namaya tua puvasan a djukulen.
S.PHON: aiʦu a puvaquan inika namaja tua puvasan a ɉukuɭən.
S.FORM: aicu a puvaquan inika namaya tua puvasan a djukulen.
S.PHON: aiʦu a puvaquan inika namaja tua puvasan a ɉukuɭən.
S.TRANSL: In millet fields we don’t beat the soil as in taro fields.

S.FORM: quɫitsan anga sakamaya sa tjuguti.
S.PHON: quʎiʦan aŋa sakamaja sa cuguti.
S.FORM: quljican anga sakamaya sa tjuguti.
S.PHON: quʎiʦan aŋa sakamaja sa ʦuguti.
S.TRANSL: We just clear away the vegetation and sow the millet.

S.FORM: aitsu a pusutjavan mamaw katua puvasan a siqusulan a mumaɫ tua upu aitsu a pusutjavan.
S.PHON: aiʦu a pusucavan mamaw katua puvasan a siqusuɭan a mumaʎ tua upu aiʦu a pusucavan.
S.FORM: aicu a pusutjavan mamaw katua puvasan a siqusulan a mumalj tua upu aicu a pusutjavan.
S.PHON: aiʦu a pusuʦavan mamaw katua puvasan a siqusuɭan a mumaʎ tua upu aiʦu a pusuʦavan.
S.TRANSL: PEANUTS Peanut fields are like taro fields; the terrace walls are renewed when the roots are dug out.

S.FORM: a puvasan maretimaɫimaɫi a sitaleman.
S.PHON: a puvasan marətimaʎimaʎi a sitaɭəman.
S.FORM: a puvasan maretimaljimalji a sitaleman.
S.PHON: a puvasan marətimaʎimaʎi a sitaɭəman.
S.TRANSL: There are different times for planting taro fields,

S.FORM: mapalalailaing a kinaqaɫaqaɫan.
S.PHON: mapaɭaɭaiɭaiŋ a kinaqaʎaqaʎan.
S.FORM: mapalalailaing a kinaqaljaqaljan.
S.PHON: mapaɭaɭaiɭaiŋ a kinaqaʎaqaʎan.
S.TRANSL: depending on the village.

S.FORM: a zuma nu qaɫan temalem nu 1, 2 gatsu.
S.PHON: a zuma nu qaʎan təmaɭəm nu *, * gaʦu.
S.FORM: a zuma nu qaljan temalem nu 1, 2 gacu.
S.PHON: a zuma nu qaʎan təmaɭəm nu *, * gaʦu.
S.TRANSL: Some villages plant in January or February;

S.FORM: a zuma nu qaɫan temalem anga nu 3, 4, 5 gatsu.
S.PHON: a zuma nu qaʎan təmaɭəm aŋa nu *, *, * gaʦu.
S.FORM: a zuma nu qaljan temalem anga nu 3, 4, 5 gacu.
S.PHON: a zuma nu qaʎan təmaɭəm aŋa nu *, *, * gaʦu.
S.TRANSL: others in March to May.

S.FORM: aitsu a sitaleman kemasi tua pinutsaviɫan a patje 5 gatsu.
S.PHON: aiʦu a sitaɭəman kəmasi tua pinuʦaviʎan a pacə * gaʦu.
S.FORM: aicu a sitaleman kemasi tua pinucaviljan a patje 5 gacu.
S.PHON: aiʦu a sitaɭəman kəmasi tua pinuʦaviʎan a paʦə * gaʦu.
S.TRANSL: Planting goes on from New Year to May.

S.FORM: aitsu a puvasan pataudan tua puk tua djulis.
S.PHON: aiʦu a puvasan pataudan tua puk tua ɉuɭis.
S.FORM: aicu a puvasan pataudan tua puk tua djulis.
S.PHON: aiʦu a puvasan pataudan tua puk tua ɉuɭis.
S.TRANSL: We also plant tree-beans and goose-foot in taro fields.

S.FORM: nu nanguaq a kadjunangan, pataudan tua ɫapanay tua qarizang a temalem.
S.PHON: nu naŋuaq a kaɉunaŋan, pataudan tua ʎapanaj tua qarizaŋ a təmaɭəm.
S.FORM: nu nanguaq a kadjunangan, pataudan tua ljapanay tua qarizang a temalem.
S.PHON: nu naŋuaq a kaɉunaŋan, pataudan tua ʎapanaj tua qarizaŋ a təmaɭəm.
S.TRANSL: If the soil is good we also plant maize or green-beans.

S.FORM: nu 11, 12, 1 gatsu kivasa.
S.PHON: nu **, **, * gaʦu kivasa.
S.FORM: nu 11, 12, 1 gacu kivasa.
S.PHON: nu **, **, * gaʦu kivasa.
S.TRANSL: We harvest taro in November to January.

S.FORM: a puvaquan nu 2, 3 gatsu, nu maviliɫ 4 gatsu, nu matalu 1 gatsu a sitjugutan.
S.PHON: a puvaquan nu *, * gaʦu, nu maviɭiʎ * gaʦu, nu mataɭu * gaʦu a sicugutan.
S.FORM: a puvaquan nu 2, 3 gacu, nu mavililj 4 gacu, nu matalu 1 gacu a sitjugutan.
S.PHON: a puvaquan nu *, * gaʦu, nu maviɭiʎ * gaʦu, nu mataɭu * gaʦu a siʦugutan.
S.TRANSL: Millet fields are planted in February or March, or April for late planting, or January for early planting.

S.FORM: qau pataudan tua ɫumay tua puk tua qarizang tua djulis tua ɫangga tua ɫapanay.
S.PHON: qau pataudan tua ʎumaj tua puk tua qarizaŋ tua ɉuɭis tua ʎaŋga tua ʎapanaj.
S.FORM: qau pataudan tua ljumay tua puk tua qarizang tua djulis tua ljangga tua ljapanay.
S.PHON: qau pataudan tua ʎumaj tua puk tua qarizaŋ tua ɉuɭis tua ʎaŋga tua ʎapanaj.
S.TRANSL: We mix in barnyard millet, tree-beans, green-beans, goose-foot, sesame(?) or maize.

S.FORM: nu 7, 8 gatsu kivaqu.
S.PHON: nu *, * gaʦu kivaqu.
S.FORM: nu 7, 8 gacu kivaqu.
S.PHON: nu *, * gaʦu kivaqu.
S.TRANSL: We harvest millet in July or August.

S.FORM: a pusutjavan 3, 4 gatsu a sitaletaleman i vurasian, sa talemi tua ɫapanay qarizang tua puk ɫangga.
S.PHON: a pusucavan *, * gaʦu a sitaɭətaɭəman i vurasian, sa taɭəmi tua ʎapanaj qarizaŋ tua puk ʎaŋga.
S.FORM: a pusutjavan 3, 4 gacu a sitaletaleman i vurasian, sa talemi tua ljapanay qarizang tua puk ljangga.
S.PHON: a pusuʦavan *, * gaʦu a sitaɭətaɭəman i vurasian, sa taɭəmi tua ʎapanaj qarizaŋ tua puk ʎaŋga.
S.TRANSL: A peanut field is planted in March or April in a sweet-potato field, and is planted with maize, green-beans, tree-beans or sesame.

S.FORM: nu 8, 9 gatsu kisutjaven.
S.PHON: nu *, * gaʦu kisucavən.
S.FORM: nu 8, 9 gacu kisutjaven.
S.PHON: nu *, * gaʦu kisuʦavən.
S.TRANSL: We harvest peanuts in August or September.

S.FORM: a puvurasian a pusavidan inika patautaudan a
S.PHON: a puvurasian a pusavidan inika patautaudan a
S.FORM: a puvurasian a pusavidan inika patautaudan a
S.PHON: a puvurasian a pusavidan inika patautaudan a
S.TRANSL: temalem.
